sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 33 - Determining Minimum CARVM Reserves for Individual Annuity Contracts.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 33 - Determining Minimum CARVM Reserves for Individual Annuity Contracts with Elective Benefits

observedSectionWindows.note: Background, purpose, benefit classification, and integrated benefit stream foundation.
Examples, valuation-interest-rate mechanics, guarantee duration, purchase rates, practical considerations, and effective-date closeout.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 33 PDF.
Do not widen into AG 34 or later guideline files.
Keep the elective-benefit / integrated-benefit-stream context explicit in every batch artifact.
Keep the page image as the backstop for exact wording because the text layer is encoding-noisy.
Treat the guideline as review-only regulatory guidance, not learner-facing policy text.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Benefit classification and integrated benefit stream foundation

topicMap.label: Mechanics, practical considerations, and closeout
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the three-page mechanics window together and leave AG 34 for a later plan.

proposedBatches.title: AG 33 benefit-classification and integrated-stream foundation slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the title, purpose, benefit-classification, and integrated-benefit-stream foundation before the mechanics window begins.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the title, purpose, benefit-classification, and integrated-benefit-stream foundation together before the mechanics window begins.

proposedBatches.title: AG 33 mechanics and closeout slice

pageTarget.notes: Capture the examples, valuation-interest-rate mechanics, guarantee duration, purchase rates, practical considerations, and effective-date closeout.

proposedBatches.rationale: Keep the examples, valuation-interest-rate mechanics, guarantee duration, purchase-rate guidance, practical considerations, and effective-date closeout together as one review-only mechanics slice.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the CARVM reserve rules as regulatory text when the guideline directs how reserves should be determined.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat elective benefits, non-elective benefits, integrated benefit streams, plan type, guarantee duration, and change in fund basis as terminology or context when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.reserveMethodStructure: Treat the integrated-benefit-stream framework, plan-type logic, and reserve formulation as reserve-method structure when the text is describing the method rather than the specific numeric outcome.
reviewStandards.calculationStructure: Treat the examples, purchase-rate guidance, and reserve-setting sequences as calculation structure when they specify how the method is applied.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat references to greatest present value, discounting, accumulation funds, and valuation-interest-rate bands as formula context unless the text gives an explicit computational rule.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat SVL-prescribed mortality, incidence, and interest-related inputs as prescribed assumptions when they are stated as fixed or table-driven requirements.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat company or industry experience language as company/prudent-estimate context when the text expressly allows or recommends it.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, the 1980 amendments, specific sections, and the AG 34 boundary as cross-reference context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title, introduction, examples, and practical-considerations paragraphs as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the page 1-3 and page 4-6 windows as review-only slices and do not split them unless a later cleanup pass explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the encoded page, elective/non-elective classification edge cases, and example-driven judgment calls as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 33 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active.
Each planned batch must have a matching batch definition.
The AG 33 batch definition file must stay synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag33-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-115 and batch-116 slices when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 33 definition file so the shared runner can find batch-115 and batch-116.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 33 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the earlier guideline batches.

notes: Controlled AG 33 planning artifact. Planned batches 115 and 116 remain review-only by default and are not extraction runs.
